attacks.text: Take a {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Being exposed to sunlight makes its movements swifter. It uses vines more adeptly than its hands.
It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

abilities.text: Each {G} Energy attached to your {G} Pokémon provides 2 {G} Energy. This effect doesn't stack.

flavorText: It only gives its all against strong opponents who are not fazed by the glare from Serperior’s noble eyes.
Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull’s head. The caps regrow overnight.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra {G} Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can’t run properly when it’s newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.
This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.

attacks.text: Discard 2 {R} Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a {W} Energy from 1 of your Benched {W} Pokémon to your Active {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.
The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.
Its large front tooth is still growing in. When the tooth itches, this Pokémon will bite another Chewtle’s horn, and the two Pokémon will tussle.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Benched {L} Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.
Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It’s soaking up static electricity because it can’t produce a charge on its own.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.
Draw a card.

flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.
If this Pokémon stands near a TV, strange scenery will appear on the screen. That scenery is said to be from its home.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Whenever a Beheeyem visits a farm, a Dubwool mysteriously disappears.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: This Pokémon can draw forth the power hidden within blooming wild flowers. It is particularly fond of red flowers.

attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon draws forth what power is left in withered flowers to make them healthy again. It holds a red flower.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon creates an impressive flower garden in its territory. It draws forth the power of the red flowers around its neck.
It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn’t get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.
By taking in a person’s scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It’s hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.
It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.
Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.
It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

flavorText: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

abilities.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it’s imitating.
The elemental composition of the rocks that form its body were found to match the bedrock of a land far away from this Pokémon’s habitat.

attacks.text: Put 1 random Koffing from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.
It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.
Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.
Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: This Pokémon spins itself rapidly and charges into its opponents. Its top speed is just over 60 mph.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claws’ toxic spikes.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: Druddigon lives in caves, but it never skips sunbathing—it won’t be able to move if its body gets too cold.
A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.
The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

trainer.text:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto their Bench.
Look at the top card of your deck. If that card is a {P} Pokémon, put it into your hand. If it is not a {P} Pokémon, put it on the bottom of your deck.
Put your Mew ex in the Active Spot into your hand.
During your opponent's next turn, all of your Pokémon take −10 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
During this turn, the Retreat Cost of your Active Pokémon is 2 less.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
